name: Hapa Protocol Standards Consolidation
summary: Consolidates Hapa's UI, API, CLI, testing, documentation, inter-node, agent, Second Brain, Hermes, avatar, and Love/Truth/Conviction standards into one reusable operating contract.

steps.label: Reference docs and app code provide API, validation, topology, UI, and Love/Truth/Conviction standards.
steps.explanation: hapa-dev-proto carries the local debug API, cluster topology, validation discipline, parity spine cards, and the Love/Truth/Conviction framing that anchors the protocol stack.

steps.label: Canon pages provide node app standards, Overwatch practices, Flow Explainer rules, and wiki schema.
steps.explanation: The wiki turns scattered operating notes into durable pages with statuses, frontmatter, source links, failure modes, and future story mechanics.

steps.label: Second Brain defines context, sharpening, sync, lineage, agents, node skills, and capability bridge standards.
steps.explanation: The retrieval layer is not just search. It records context purpose, fit, feedback, creation lineage, agent notes, node-space activity, and deployment opportunities.

steps.label: Hermes contributes long-running agent and harness standards.
steps.explanation: Hermes profiles, skills, memory, schedules, gateways, tools, and backups set expectations for delegated agent work with explicit capability and writeback checks.

steps.label: Avatar nodes contribute identity, lineage, generated interpretation, and 3D prep standards.
steps.explanation: Avatar work must preserve source media, generated variants, reviewed facts, lineage manifests, and Trellis-safe source decisions before downstream production.

steps.label: The standard becomes a generated protocol-flow JSON and registry entry.
steps.explanation: App storage makes the standard discoverable in Node Space and available to future flow explainers.

steps.label: The standard becomes an indexed article and enrichment target.
steps.explanation: Second Brain can now retrieve, connect, and redeploy the consolidated standard in later Hapa work.

card.description: Turns scattered Hapa operating rules into one source-linked standards spine.
card.effect: Future node work can inherit UI/API/CLI parity, validation output, documentation, interoperability, agent, avatar, and sharpening expectations.
card.risk: Standards that are not enforced can become lore-only. Each active node still needs a parity check and gap ticket.
